new_classes.definition_en: A sparkling wine with a protected designation of origin (PDO), produced from grapes of the 'Glera' vine variety harvested in the regions of the designations of origin 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', and 'Asolo — Prosecco'.
A quality borne by a wine indicating that it is produced exclusively from grapes of the 'Glera' vine variety, as required for classification under CN 22041015 (Prosecco PDO).

A quality borne by a sparkling wine indicating that it originates from one of the protected designations of origin 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', or 'Asolo — Prosecco', as required for classification under CN 22041015.

coverage_explanation: The three primary distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) Prosecco is a sparkling wine (subClassOf SparklingWine), (2) it must be produced from Glera grape variety grapes (GlераGrapeVarietalQuality restriction), and (3) it must carry a protected designation of origin from the four named Italian PDO regions (ProseccoPDOOriginQuality restriction). A sibling disjointness with Champagne is added since both are named PDO sparkling wines under the same CN subheading parent. Coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) the four specific PDO region names ('Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', 'Asolo — Prosecco') are enumerated in the legal text but cannot be individually represented as named individuals without introducing SKOS/SKOSXL region individuals not present in the TBox; (b) the production process specifics (secondary fermentation method characteristic of Prosecco DOC/DOCG) are not explicitly stated in the legal note and hence not modelled.
